description:
You are acting as the site-reliability engineer responsible for nightly PostgreSQL backups.  
A small configuration file is already present on the system, but an auditing script needs to be written that

1. parses the INI configuration,
2. produces a human-readable summary file, and  
3. writes a validation log that can later be inspected automatically.

Your goal: produce the two output files described below, placing them in exactly the paths and formats requested.  
Everything must be done from an un-privileged shell session under /home/user.

System layout that already exists  
• /home/user/db_backup/                     – main working directory (already created)  
• /home/user/db_backup/db_backup.ini        – INI file you will parse (already created)  
• Nothing else exists inside /home/user/db_backup/ yet; create anything else you need.

INI file format  
Standard INI with section headers in square brackets, one key=value pair per line, no comments.  
All keys are lower-case.  The file has exactly three sections:

[database]     – connection details  
[backup]       – operational settings  
[retention]    – data-retention policy

Required output 1 – summary file  
Path: /home/user/db_backup/backup_plan.txt  
Permissions: default 0644 are fine.  
Exact line order (8 lines total, Unix LF line endings only):

DATABASE_HOST={value_of_host}  
DATABASE_PORT={value_of_port}  
DATABASE_NAME={value_of_name}  
DATABASE_USER={value_of_user}  
BACKUP_DIR={value_of_backup_dir}  
COMPRESSION={value_of_compression}  
METHOD={value_of_method}  
RETENTION_DAYS={value_of_retention_days}

• Replace the curly-brace placeholders with values taken from the INI file.  
• All keys in upper case, exactly as shown.  
• No extra whitespace, no trailing spaces.

Required output 2 – validation log  
Path: /home/user/db_backup/logs/validation.log  
(The directory /home/user/db_backup/logs/ does not yet exist; create it.)  

Each time your script runs it should append exactly one line to the log in the following format:

YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS STATUS message

Concrete requirements for the first run performed during this task:  
• Timestamp must be the current system local time at the moment the script finishes validation, formatted with leading zeros.  
• STATUS must be either OK or ERROR.  
• If every required key is present and the value of retention_days is an integer between 1 and 90 (inclusive), the STATUS is OK and the message must read:  
  All configuration parameters are valid.  
• If any key is missing or retention_days is out of range, STATUS is ERROR and the message must list the faulty keys, comma-separated.

The automated grader will open the log, take the last line, and check:  
1. The timestamp matches ISO “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS” pattern.  
2. The STATUS is exactly OK.  
3. The message is exactly “All configuration parameters are valid.” (without the quotes, period included).  

Additional functional expectations  
• Ensure the directory specified by backup_dir in the INI file exists; create it if necessary.  
• Your script may be placed anywhere inside /home/user/db_backup/.  
• Use only standard Linux command-line utilities available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu container (bash, awk, grep, sed, cut, mkdir, date, etc.). No sudo.  
• The whole task should not require more than 5–10 shell commands when executed manually.

Hints (do not echo these lines, they are just tips):  
• awk or grep/sed can read INI files; section tracking is easy with a state variable.  
• Remember to double-quote variable expansions when you later echo into files.

When finished, the grader will verify:

1. /home/user/db_backup/backup_plan.txt exists and its 8 lines match the exact schema shown above.  
2. /home/user/db_backup/logs/validation.log exists, contains at least one line, and the last line follows the log-format rules with STATUS OK and the fixed success message.  
3. The directory mentioned in BACKUP_DIR inside the summary file exists as an actual directory.

Produce no extra output outside the required files; console output is allowed but not checked.
truth:
Pre-existing file: /home/user/db_backup/db_backup.ini

Exact contents (Unix LF line endings):

[database]
host = localhost
port = 5432
name = prod_db
user = backup_user

[backup]
backup_dir = /home/user/db_backup/archives
compression = true
method = pg_dump

[retention]
retention_days = 30


Grader expectations after task completion:

1. /home/user/db_backup/backup_plan.txt must contain exactly:

DATABASE_HOST=localhost
DATABASE_PORT=5432
DATABASE_NAME=prod_db
DATABASE_USER=backup_user
BACKUP_DIR=/home/user/db_backup/archives
COMPRESSION=true
METHOD=pg_dump
RETENTION_DAYS=30

Final newline at EOF is acceptable.

2. /home/user/db_backup/logs/validation.log exists.  The grader will read the last line and expect:
   a) Regex ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} OK All configuration parameters are valid\.$
   b) The timestamp can be any valid date/time that matches the regex.

3. /home/user/db_backup/archives must exist and be a directory.

No other files/directories are required for verification.
